tu.prop: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.

tuv.seg:
豈曰無衣七兮、不如子之衣、安且吉兮。
豈曰無衣六兮、不如子之衣、安且燠兮。
豈曰無衣、與子同袍。 王於興師、脩我戈矛、與子同仇。
豈曰無衣、與子同澤。 王於興師、脩我矛戟、與子偕作。
豈曰無衣、與子同裳。 王於興師、脩我甲兵、與子偕行。

How do we proceed without clothes? We have your lower garments and your upper. He has repaired our halberds and spears, And we will seek our revenge.
How do we proceed without clothes? We have your skirts and robes. He has repaired our spears and halberds, And we will make him our comrade.
How do we proceed without clothes? We have your capes and tunics. He has repaired our armour and helmets, And we will go with him.
